title: REST Architecture and Principles: Foundational Philosophy

timestamps.label: The Paradox of Modern Software Engineering
Backend Testing: Inspecting Plumbing in the Dark
Historical Context: Roy Fielding and SOAP
REST as an Architectural Style: The Restaurant Analogy
Impact on QA: Headless and Shift-Left Testing
Statelessness Mechanics: Carts and Auth Tokens
Disasters: Race Conditions and Parallel Testing
Constraint 4: Uniform Interface and Representational State
Conclusion: AI Agents and the Future of State

vocabulary.definition: Representational State Transfer — an architectural style for distributed hypermedia systems.
The architectural constraint where the server retains no client session context between requests.
The property of certain operations where executing them multiple times has the same effect as executing them once.
JSON Web Token — a self-contained identity document consisting of header, payload, and signature.
The process of separating software components so they can evolve and scale independently.

short_answer.en: REST is an architectural style based on six constraints: client-server separation, statelessness, cacheability, uniform interface, layered system, and code on demand. It focuses on decoupling components to achieve global scalability and resilience.
short_answer.ru: REST — это архитектурный стиль, основанный на шести ограничениях: разделение клиента и сервера, отсутствие состояния, кэшируемость, единообразие интерфейса, многоуровневая система и код по запросу. Он фокусируется на разделении компонентов для достижения глобальной масштабируемости и устойчивости.
